meaning: n.一百五十周年纪念adj.一百五十[周]年的
examples: And it is the sesquicentennial of the book next year, along with the bicentennial of Darwin 's birth, which will be more widely marked than the 1858 event.
Compare Montgomery’s centennial with the sesquicentennial, which this February drew a ragtag few hundred enthusiasts (and no elected officials) to parade through Montgomery.
So, in 2004, cities from St Paul to Davenport in Iowa plan to celebrate the sesquicentennial of the Grand Excursion by doing it all over again.
January 1, 2013 marks the sesquicentennial of the Emancipation Proclamation.
